objective: Run FigOps through a repeatable spec-review-implementation-verification loop focused on productization, operability, maintainability, release alignment, and publication-quality figure review.

loop.iteration_rule: Do not implement a slice until acceptance criteria and verification commands are documented.
loop.fan_in_rule: Every iteration must end with confirmed, refuted, unverified, and residual-risk notes.

lanes.goal: Make first-run and source-checkout environment failures obvious and actionable.
acceptance: Installed-package and source-checkout quickstarts are clearly separated.
Missing uv, pytest, pandas, matplotlib, or Rscript is reported as an environment blocker, not a product pass.
Doctor hints remain actionable on Windows, WSL/Linux, and macOS.

lanes.goal: Keep large-module debt visible and reduce it through behavior-preserving slices.
acceptance: Architecture docs distinguish pytest-checked inventory freshness from policy-only import layering.
Any extraction preserves compatibility imports unless explicitly deprecated.
Moved behavior has a witness test.

lanes.goal: Keep public release claims true when source and published package state differ.
acceptance: Source version and locally documented published release are not conflated.
Network-verified latest release claims are only made after explicit verification.

lanes.goal: Define publication-ready figure quality beyond hard geometry and journal checks.
acceptance: A compact review rubric separates hard gates from advisory polish.
Future diagnostics can map to rubric items.
Rubric examples cover visual hierarchy, contrast, label density, panel balance, and traceability.

iterations.name: Productization loop and architecture truth alignment
scope: Create productization loop spec and plan.
Correct architecture and roadmap language around module-size inventory CI coverage.
verification: python3 hub_uv.py run python -m pytest tests/test_architecture_inventory.py -q
iterations.environment_policy: If pytest or uv is unavailable, record the blocker and run the dependency-light py_compile command.

iterations.name: Environment and install readiness
scope: Improve first-run guidance for installed-package and source-checkout users.
Keep doctor output actionable when uv, pytest, runtime dependencies, or Rscript are unavailable.
iterations.environment_policy: If pytest is unavailable, record the blocker and run py_compile for touched Python files.

iterations.name: Release and version truth alignment
scope: Check public-facing release claims for consistency between source 0.17.10 and locally documented public release 0.17.9.
Avoid network-latest claims unless explicitly verified.
non_goals: No network verification without explicit approval.
verification: rg -n "0\\.17\\.9|0\\.17\\.10|latest|published|PyPI|GitHub Release" README.md CHANGELOG.md docs/packaging docs/ROADMAP.md
iterations.environment_policy: If release scripts require unavailable dependencies, record the blocker and use text inspection as the minimum check.

iterations.name: Publication-ready figure quality rubric
scope: Define hard gates versus advisory polish for rendered figure review.
Map rubric items to existing FigOps checks where possible.
iterations.environment_policy: This slice should remain dependency-light and documentation-only.

iterations.name: Lazy facade and doctor lightweight regression lock
scope: Lock the `hub_core` and `hub_core.mcp` lazy facade behavior with dependency-light import hygiene tests.
Prove `figops_mcp_server.py doctor --json` can report missing runtime dependencies without importing heavy MCP schema/theme modules first.
non_goals: No full MCP render smoke in a dependency-light shell.
No change to public tool names or compatibility aliases.
verification: python3 -m py_compile tests/test_doctor.py tests/test_public_api_import_hygiene.py hub_core/__init__.py hub_core/mcp/__init__.py hub_core/doctor.py graphhub_mcp_server.py figops_mcp_server.py
iterations.environment_policy: Use unittest/py_compile and a guarded subprocess scenario when pytest or uv is unavailable.

iterations.name: Dependency-light source checkout minimum gate
scope: Define and exercise the stdlib-only source-checkout checks that can run before uv and pytest are available.
Keep `hub_uv.py --help` truthful on machines where the executable is `python3` rather than `python`.
non_goals: No replacement for pytest or full regression.
No change to uv-managed runtime layout.
iterations.environment_policy: Treat pytest failure as an environment blocker when `pytest` is not installed; unittest remains the minimum dependency-light gate.

iterations.name: uv-backed gitflow verification pass
scope: Install uv into the user-local tool path after explicit user approval.
Run focused pytest, MCP smoke, public release checks, ruff, and full pytest through `hub_uv.py`.
Move the dirty worktree off `main` onto a productization verification branch without committing or reverting unrelated changes.
non_goals: No broad lint cleanup across unrelated decomposition work.
verification: PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" python3 hub_uv.py run python -m pytest tests/test_doctor.py tests/test_public_api_import_hygiene.py tests/test_uv_runtime.py -q
PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" python3 hub_uv.py run python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_read_only.py tests/test_mcp_rendering.py tests/test_mcp_batch_quality.py -q
PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" python3 hub_uv.py run python scripts/check_public_release.py --root .
PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" python3 hub_uv.py run python -m pytest -q
iterations.environment_policy: User-local uv installation is allowed by this explicit user request; shell profile PATH persistence remains untouched.

human_gates: network claims about latest public release
